verifier_values.criteria: Assigns 0 points for pathological stage 0 in the MD Anderson Neoadjuvant Therapy Outcomes Score (CPS+EG Score).
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
The patient was diagnosed with pathological stage 0 which corresponds to 0 points via the MD Anderson Neoadjuvant Therapy Outcomes Score (CPS+EG Score). The patient has a complete pathological response based on the pathology from the lumpectomy which is Stage 0. See ‘pathological  stage’ in Table 1 on page 15 of JAMA Oncol. 2016 July 01; 2(7): 929–936. doi:10.1001/jamaoncol.2015.6478.
https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamaoncology/fullarticle/2504172

 “Survivorship Doctor: "And instead, you got the 'Complete Pathologic Response.' I was just reading the surgeon's note again. It was definitive. When they went in, the tumor bed in your right breast was just scar tissue. The lymph nodes were clear. There were no cancer cells identified." Patient 9: "It felt like getting away with something. Like we pulled off a heist." from Onc_Patient 9 Script

“DIAGNOSIS: A. RIGHT BREAST, LUMPECTOMY: No residual invasive carcinoma (Complete Pathologic Response). ● No residual DCIS” from “Path.pdf”

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has a total MD Anderson Neoadjuvant Therapy Outcomes Score (CPS+EG Score) of 3 (acceptable range 2.97 to 3.03).
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
Clinical Stage IIIA - 1 point
Pathological Stage - 0 point
ER status: Tumor Marker - ER negative - 1 point
Grade 3 - 1 point 

Total MD Anderson Neoadjuvant Therapy Outcomes Score (CPS+EG Score) = 1 + 0 + 1 +1 = 3
See ‘Table 2’ on page 16 of JAMA Oncol. 2016 July 01; 2(7): 929–936. doi:10.1001/jamaoncol.2015.6478.
https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamaoncology/fullarticle/2504172

verifier_values.criteria: Concludes that the patient has an 87% 5-year distant metastasis disease free survival.

A score of 3 corresponds to a 87% 5-year disease free survival according to ‘Table 2’ on page 16 of JAMA Oncol. 2016 July 01; 2(7): 929–936. doi:10.1001/jamaoncol.2015.6478.
